Home World Business Sports

Coastal town reopens after long storm

By Test Writer · September 12, 2024

The seaside village of Aldermouth reopened its harbor on Wednesday after a week of high winds and torrential rain that had grounded fishing boats and closed the seafront promenade.

Local officials described damage to the harbor wall as moderate, with several sections of the protective barrier needing replacement before the autumn storm season.

Residents said they were relieved that the worst of the weather had passed. The harbor master said inspections would continue throughout the week.

Recovery efforts

Volunteer teams have been working since dawn to clear debris from the main thoroughfares. The local council expects the cleanup to be complete by the weekend.

Power had been restored to all affected homes by Tuesday evening, although a small number of properties on the cliff road were still being assessed for structural safety.
